config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama; kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh dipakai untuk mengorbankan kebebasan dan martabat orang secara semena-mena. Dalam pengalaman saya, kalau “kepentingan bersama” ditempatkan terlalu tinggi tanpa kontrol, itu gampang dipakai untuk membenarkan ketidakadilan. Yang ideal adalah mencari keseimbangan, bukan menjadikan individu selalu kalah oleh mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual’s rights. From an American perspective, I value constitutional rights precisely because majorities can be wrong, and “the common good” has too often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, or abuse of power. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every case, because public safety and basic order do matter, but the burden should be on society to justify any limit very carefully. In my view, the default should be that the individual comes first unless there is a clear, narrow, and necessary reason to restrict that right.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis berada di atas hak individu. Pengalaman di Indonesia justru menunjukkan bahwa alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa, jadi saya tidak percaya mayoritas selalu lebih benar. Memang ada keadaan tertentu seperti keselamatan publik atau bencana, tetapi pembatasan hak harus ketat, jelas, dan sementara, bukan dijadikan dalih umum. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang membuat individu selalu kalah, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak orang sambil menjaga kepentingan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From a U.S. perspective, that idea has a dangerous history because governments and majorities often claim to act for the common good while silencing dissent, discriminating against minorities, or expanding power beyond what is justified. At the same time, I do not think rights are unlimited in every situation, since laws for public safety and preventing harm are legitimate, but they should be narrow, temporary, and closely scrutinized. The default should be that society has to prove why any individual right should be limited, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Dari pengalaman Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan kritik, mengabaikan minoritas, atau menormalisasi penyalahgunaan kuasa, jadi saya tidak mau memberi cek kosong pada mayoritas. Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti bencana atau ancaman keselamatan publik, tetapi pembatasan hak harus sangat ketat, jelas, dan sementara, bukan dijadikan pembenaran permanen. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru yang mampu melindungi hak individu sambil mencari kepentingan bersama, bukan yang membuat individu selalu kalah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule, because in the United States we have too many examples of “the public good” being used to justify abuses that later looked indefensible. The point about emergencies is real, but that is exactly why limits on rights have to be narrow, temporary, and closely checked rather than assumed to be good just because a majority wants them. I do not trust the idea that the collective is usually wiser than the individual, especially when minorities are the ones most likely to pay the price. A healthy society protects individual rights first and then asks when, if ever, a specific exception is truly necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
